title: CLI --json double-encodes structured op output (output is a stringified JSON string, not a JSON value)
description: Surfaced by dogfooding the CLI. Under --json the envelope at packages/pi-context-cli/src/cli.ts:487 emits {ok, op, output: text} where text is the op run() return. OpDefinition.run returns a string, and roughly 27 data-returning ops produce JSON as a string (via JSON.stringify or serializeForRead().content). So the output field under --json is a stringified JSON string, forcing consumers to double-parse: JSON.parse(JSON.parse(stdout).output). The roughly 21 prose-returning ops are unaffected. No test asserts the --json output shape, so the double-encode was never caught. Agent-investigated for root cause, shape, and reproducible conditions before filing.

evidence.reference: envelope wraps the op string return; a JSON-returning op double-encodes under --json
resolve-item-by-id / read-block-item / read-config run() return JSON.stringify(...) or serializeForRead().content, i.e. a string

impact: A machine consumer of --json must double-parse every structured op, which defeats the point of --json as machine-parseable output. The CLI surface the cli arc built is harder to consume programmatically and the inconsistency is silent (no test locks the shape).
proposed_resolution: Under --json, emit the op structured payload as a JSON value rather than an embedded JSON string: either the OpDefinition.run contract distinguishes a structured return from prose, or the envelope detects JSON-returning ops and parses-then-embeds. Add a test asserting --json output is a JSON value for a structured op. Repro: pi-context resolve-item-by-id --id TASK-009 --json shows output as a string requiring a second parse.
